excerpt: Every Imam embodies four divine realities corresponding to the four words of the Basmala
source_authority: High confidence in Fatimid theological corpus
source_location_hint: On the structure of spiritual stations in relation to divine attributes

excerpt: No obligatory prayer exceeds four rak'ahs, and none falls below two rak'ahs, for these manifest the outward and inward stations of the Imam
source_authority: Foundational jurisprudential text of Ismaili law
source_location_hint: On the structure and regulation of obligatory prayers

excerpt: The Speaker is called obligatory Imam because God made him a Prophet; the Proof through the Messenger's command; the Imam through the Guardian's command
source_location_hint: On the hierarchy of spiritual offices and their scriptural foundations

excerpt: The supererogatory prayer is also called the voluntary (nāfila), signifying descendant of the descendant
source_location_hint: On the classification of supererogatory acts and spiritual kinship

excerpt: The two rak'ahs of Dawn refer to the two supreme limits of the spiritual realm: the First Intellect and the universal Soul
source_location_hint: On the cosmological hierarchy and the role of intellectual principles in prayer

excerpt: The Basmala contains nineteen letters, corresponding to the seven Imams and twelve Proofs
source_authority: Ismaili exegetical work on divine unity
source_location_hint: On numerical correspondences in sacred language and cosmology

excerpt: The limits of prayer are seven, equal to the number of the seven Imams and seven Proofs. Four are obligatory and three are recommended.
source_location_hint: On the seven ritual and inner limits of prayer

excerpt: Prayer is not offered at the moment of zenith because at that time the influences of Saturn and Mars are dominant
source_location_hint: On the astronomical and spiritual significance of prayer times

excerpt: The Afternoon Prayer rests on the Qa'im, who will squeeze out all sects and spread Islam throughout the world
source_location_hint: On the Qa'im's role in the final age and the meaning of the Afternoon Prayer

excerpt: The seventeen obligatory rak'ahs plus two recommended rak'ahs of Dawn equal nineteen, corresponding to the nineteen words of the Basmala
source_location_hint: On sacred numerology and the relationship between the Basmala and prayer structure

excerpt: Every thousandth Imam is called Qa'im because he gathers the congregation of the final thousand years
source_location_hint: On the concept of the Qa'im and cyclical time in Ismaili cosmology
